canonical_question: What triggers a permanent establishment for tax - OECD thresholds, digital PE, risk scenarios?
aliases: When does a foreign office or remote worker create a PE?
OECD Article 5 permanent establishment triggers and thresholds
Digital permanent establishment rules and Pillar One nexus
constraints: Treaty-dependent: PE definitions vary by bilateral tax treaty - always check the specific treaty between the two countries, not just the OECD Model
Country reservations: India, Israel, Nigeria, Malaysia and others have expressed reservations to the 2025 OECD Commentary update - local rules may differ
US treaties follow the US Model, not the OECD Model - OECD Commentary is non-binding interpretive material for US treaty purposes
Digital PE / DST rules are unilateral measures adopted by 18+ countries outside the OECD Model framework - separate analysis required
Pillar One Amount A (EUR 20B revenue / 10% profit margin threshold) remains unratified as of March 2026 - not yet in force
State/provincial economic nexus rules (e.g., US state nexus thresholds of $100K-$1M+) operate independently of treaty PE rules

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking about US state tax nexus (not federal/treaty PE)
User is asking about VAT/GST registration thresholds
User is asking about transfer pricing profit allocation (not PE threshold)

inputs_needed.question: What type of activity is occurring in the foreign country?
options: Fixed office, branch, or factory (fixed place PE)
Employee or agent signing contracts (dependent agent PE)
Remote workers / home office (remote work PE)
Digital services with no physical presence (digital PE / DST)
Service delivery over extended period (service PE)

inputs_needed.question: What percentage of total working time does the individual spend in the foreign jurisdiction over a 12-month period?
options: Under 50% (generally safe harbor under 2025 OECD update)

inputs_needed.question: Is there a tax treaty between the home country and the foreign jurisdiction?
options: Yes - OECD-based bilateral treaty in force
Yes - but with known reservations to PE definitions
No treaty - domestic PE rules apply

applies_to.user_segment: Multinational enterprises, cross-border employers, tax advisors, compliance officers
applies_to.context: Determining when business activities in a foreign jurisdiction create a taxable presence (PE) under OECD Model Tax Convention Article 5 and bilateral tax treaties
